source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Anna has glued together several cubes of the same size to form a solid (see picture). Which of the following pictures shows a different view of this solid?
<image1>
<image2>

issues: question_text contains two image placeholders ('<image1>' and '<image2>'), but only one top-level image path is provided.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'see picture' and asks which picture shows a different view of the solid
task depends on recognizing 3D cube arrangement and comparing visual views, which is not recoverable from text alone
multi_step: the problem requires identifying the base cube solid and comparing it against multiple candidate views
solving likely needs spatial rotation or consistency checking across several options rather than a single direct lookup
there are 5 answer choices, increasing comparison steps
question is image-based geometry rather than a one-step textual fact recall
verifiability: answer_type is option/choice with a single recorded answer 'C'
multiple-choice output is easy to check against the gold answer

risk_flags: question_text contains two image placeholders ('<image1>' and '<image2>'), but only one top-level image path is provided.

decision_reasons: Core assets are present: question text, one image, and a gold multiple-choice answer are all available.
The sample is highly image-dependent, so the reported mismatch between two image placeholders and only one provided top-level image path creates a meaningful recovery risk.
The asset issues appear to be consistency/coverage problems rather than total loss of semantic content, so high-recall collection guidance favors low_priority over reject.
Verifiability remains reasonably strong because the answer is a single recorded choice, but follow-up is needed to confirm whether the single image fully covers both referenced visuals.
